summary: Regression test for MCP terminal-schedule guard bug. 4 RED (R1/R5 isError), 9 GREEN (R2/R3/R4/R5b). DB constraint chk_task_instances_terminal_scheduled fires before app guard; fix must add guard before DB write.

findings.evidence: Handler at L393-426 writes {status, updated_at} directly; no check for scheduled_at IS NULL before the DB write. DB constraint chk_task_instances_terminal_scheduled fires and propagates as raw MySQL error. Tests R1a/R1b/R1c confirm RED: handler throws instead of returning isError:true.
findings.fix: Add guard before updateTaskById call: if status in TERMINAL_REQUIRES_SCHEDULE AND !existing.scheduled_at AND !isRollingInstance → return isError. Mirror UpdateTaskStatus.js:147-160.

findings.claim: update_task has no terminal-requires-schedule guard when status is terminal
findings.evidence: Handler at L246-382 performs taskToRow() + updateTaskById() without checking: is status terminal AND is scheduled_at null in both existing row and the update call? DB constraint fires unhandled. Test R5a confirms RED.
findings.fix: Add guard: if fields.status in TERMINAL_REQUIRES_SCHEDULE AND !existing.scheduled_at AND !fields.date AND !fields.scheduledAt → return isError. Allow when date/scheduledAt provided in same call (R5b must still pass).

findings.claim: Broken require path in mcp.js helper prevented any test from using captureHandlers via this helper
findings.evidence: require('../src/mcp/tools/tasks') from tests/helpers/ resolves to tests/src/... (non-existent). fixedRecurringGap.test.js:22 explicitly documented this. Fixed to ../../src/mcp/tools/tasks.

findings.claim: Unscheduled rolling instances also blocked by DB constraint — not tested
findings.evidence: chk_task_instances_terminal_scheduled applies to all rows including rolling instances. App-level exemption alone is not sufficient; scheduled_at must be set. Bert must decide: set scheduled_at=NOW() for rolling instances, or document that unscheduled rolling instances cannot be terminal.
findings.fix: REFER→bert: design decision for unscheduled rolling instances on the MCP path.

refers.why: Implement guard in set_task_status and update_task before the DB write; design decision needed for unscheduled rolling instances and DB constraint satisfaction
